author: Pecked
text:
bl review: configurable pre-squash gate

balls commits the squash with --no-verify (every balls commit shares one git_commit helper), so a git pre-commit hook structurally never sees bl review's merge to the integration branch, and CI only runs once it has landed. The project's 300-line and 100%-coverage gates were unenforced at the one moment they matter — the merge. cli.rs sat at 305 lines on main as proof.

bl review now runs an optional review.pre_check command after committing the worker's work and merging the integration branch into the worktree — so the check sees the exact end-state being delivered — and before the squash (local-squash) or the branch push (deferred). A non-zero exit aborts cleanly: no squash, no push, no status flip. The integration-branch merge stays in the worktree so the agent fixes it there and retries. The check inherits stdio, streaming its output live.

Design: a config key, not a convention file — it lives in committed config, so a master_url hub enforces it federation-wide. Opt-in; unset is byte-identical to before. no-git/no-worktree review has no worktree to run in and skips the gate. No escape-hatch flag: blank the config to bypass, and a federated hub deliberately leaves no worker-side override. Fixing install-hooks.sh's bare-hub dangling symlink is a separate ball.

config.rs was exactly at the 300-line cap, so the nested config blocks (Delivery, DeliveryMode, and the new ReviewConfig) moved to config_blocks.rs, re-exported from config so config::Delivery and friends are unchanged.
